steps.title: Test ordered pair I: (1, 1)
steps.content: Substitute \(x=1, y=1\): $$5(1)-3(1)=5-3=2 < 4.$$ True. Pair I satisfies the inequality.

steps.title: Test ordered pair II: (2, 5)
steps.content: Substitute \(x=2, y=5\): $$5(2)-3(5)=10-15=-5 < 4.$$ True. Pair II satisfies the inequality.

steps.title: Test ordered pair III: (3, 2)
steps.content: Substitute \(x=3, y=2\): $$5(3)-3(2)=15-6=9 < 4.$$ False. Pair III does not satisfy the inequality.

steps.title: Conclude
steps.content: Only pairs I and II satisfy \(5x - 3y < 4\), so the answer is choice C.

explanation:
• Choice A — 'I only': Pair I works, but Pair II also works, so this is incomplete. ✗
  • Choice B — 'II only': Pair II works, but so does Pair I, so this is incomplete. ✗
  • Choice C — 'I and II only': Pair I gives 2 < 4 (true); Pair II gives −5 < 4 (true); Pair III gives 9 < 4 (false). Exactly I and II satisfy the inequality. ✓
  • Choice D — 'I and III only': Pair III gives 9 < 4, which is false, so III cannot be included. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
